summary: Set 033 Session 3 of 6: shipped the TypeScript UI affordance migration (H1 + H3 + H4) and closed the S2-shipped writer-side breakage (spec risk R1). NEW Node shim scripts/claude-session-start-invoker.js (~190 LOC) replaces the retired write-orchestrator-marker.js for the Claude SessionStart hook path — reads hook payload, walks up cwd to find docs/session-sets/<slug>/ with status: in-progress, spawns `python -m ai_router.start_session` with engine=claude + provider=anthropic and preserveExistingClaude() preserving the existing model+effort when claude+anthropic already holds (SessionStart payload has no model signal). git mv src/commands/setOrchestratorManual.ts → checkOutOrchestrator.ts (blame preserved); rewrote dispatch path: dropped HELPER_REL + helperPathAbs + dispatchManualWrite + readCurrentMarkerForWorkspace; added PROVIDER_TO_ENGINE map (anthropic→claude, openai→codex, google→gemini, github→copilot) as the H4 identity bridge with exported providerToEngine() function; added listInProgressSetsAt() walk-up resolver reading session-state.json's orchestrator block; added pickTargetInProgressSet() that prompts on multi-in-progress (multi-set rendering is first-class post-S2); dispatchCheckOut() spawns python start_session with resolved pythonPath setting; maybeConfirmForceOverride() compares (engine, provider) excluding model per H4 (R3 by design). Command id renamed dabbler.setOrchestrator → dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator; quickpick titles 'Set Orchestrator —' → 'Check Out As —'; keybinding-snippet command id updated. NEW src/commands/releaseCheckOut.ts (~85 LOC) implementing H3's named release path: shared pickTargetInProgressSet resolver, no-op short-circuit on unclaimed slot, modal confirmation naming the current holder, delegation to dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator with { targetSet } pre-populated so the operator picks the new holder in the same multi-step flow (two-modal sequence intentional — first establishes intent, second sanity-checks identity handoff). Refactored src/commands/installOrchestratorHookClaudeCode.ts: points at the new invoker; matcher-update logic accepts BOTH the new invoker filename AND the retired write-orchestrator-marker.js filename via DABBLER_HOOK_SUBSTRINGS so re-install repairs stale references; UserPromptSubmit hook retired entirely (signalKind field gone post-S2) with new pruneDabblerHooks() helper that surgically removes stale dabbler-managed entries while preserving other operator-installed hooks at the same matcher verbatim. Refactored src/codex/configWatcher.ts same way — dropped helper invocation + classifyTier + payload-shaping, added resolveSingleInProgressSet + same-holder short-circuit (avoids spamming python on every editor save) + dispatchCheckOut spawning start_session with engine=codex + provider=openai. Both invoker paths NEVER pass --force (hooks/watchers don't auto-override; operator routes explicit overrides through Release Check-Out). Gemini + Copilot installer shims updated to executeCommand the renamed dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator id. Updates rippled through providers/ActionRegistry.ts (row 501 id+label, new row 502 releaseCheckOut, openOrchestratorWriterLog bumped 502→503), providers/CustomSessionSetsView.ts (SUPPORTED_ROW_COMMAND_IDS allowlist), providers/detectOrchestrators.ts (CODEX_CTA commandId + provider CTA labels 'set ... orchestrator' → 'check out as ...'), providers/OrchestratorAccordion.ts (mismatch-reason copy), src/extension.ts (import + safeRegister rename + new releaseCheckOut registration), package.json contributes.commands (rename + new release-check-out command), media/session-sets-tree/client.js (comment). Tests: git mv setOrchestratorManual.test.ts → checkOutOrchestrator.test.ts; preserved 13 MRU+formatTupleLabel tests verbatim + added 4 new providerToEngine tests. NEW releaseCheckOut.test.ts (4 describeHolder tests). Updated actionRegistry.test.ts (ROW_ACTIONS length 16 → 17; new dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator + dabbler.releaseCheckOut in expected set; coverage of both new ids in 'appears only on in-progress rows' test) + detectOrchestrators.test.ts (Codex-only CTA expectation). D13 lint fired on three state.currentSession reads in the new code (checkOutOrchestrator listInProgressSetsAt + dispatchCheckOut, configWatcher resolveSingleInProgressSet); resolved via inline // noqa: D13 carve-outs with justifying comments — the reads pass currentSession verbatim to start_session as --session-number, not legacy-progress derivation. npx tsc --noEmit clean. npm run test:unit: 409 passing (up from 408 — +5 new tests, -1 lint fix that re-passes) and 2 pre-existing failures (configEditor-foundation + notificationsSection — unchanged from S2 disposition, neither touches changed files). Round A verification via gemini-pro $0.044 VERIFIED with no must-fix issues (single-line response 'VERIFIED: no must-fix issues.' on 109,162-char bundle covering 7 assertion groups: A H1 invoker refactor, B H3 release affordance, C H4 identity surface, D rename completeness end-to-end, E UserPromptSubmit retirement, F test adequacy, G risky/missing). Session 3 closes near top of $0.05-$0.15 forecast band; cumulative routed spend across S1+S2+S3 ≈$0.13 of $1.25 total Set 033 NTE. S3 closes spec risk R1 (write-orchestrator-marker.js HELPER_REL references retired in all three offending files); the writer-side surface and reader-side surface and UI surface now form a coherent check-out/check-in model end-to-end.

reason.specifics: Session 4 is the Layer-3 Playwright coverage of operator-visible behaviors introduced by S1+S2+S3: (1) multi-in-progress rendering with two distinct orchestrator.engine values; (2) check-out refusal scenario asserting stderr contains the holder's engine+provider + the literal substrings '--force' and 'Release Check-Out'; (3) force-override scenario asserting writer-log appends prior+new holder + ISO timestamp; (4) Command Palette Release Check-Out scenario asserting the orchestrator block transitions correctly; (5) same-orchestrator re-attach asserting checkedOutAt unchanged + lastActivityAt bumped. The rename groundwork S3 just shipped (dabbler.releaseCheckOut + new shim) is the operator-visible surface scenarios (3) and (4) will exercise directly. Same model + effort holds: high-effort is right because Playwright scenario authoring against the multi-set fixture path + Electron launch env pollution gotchas requires careful fixture scaffolding (project_electron_launch_env_pollution + project_playwright_electron_works_on_windows memories), and a missed scenario reduces R2/R6 risk coverage downstream.
